title: Head trialling system
abstract: A trial mobile bearing (16) configured to removably capture a generally convex head component (18), the bearing comprising a spherical cap comprising: a convex outer surface (20) configured to engage with generally concave inner bearing surface (24) of a liner component (14), a concave inner surface (24) configured to removably capture the generally convex head component, a pole (26), an equator (28); a rim (30), and a plurality of resilient leaves (32) circumferentially separated by through-slits (34) extending longitudinally from the rim towards the pole.
claims:
Claims 1. A trial mobile bearing configured to removably capture a generally convex head component, the bearing comprising: a spherical hollow cap comprising a convex outer surface configured to engage with a generally concave inner bearing surface of a liner component; a concave inner surface configured to removably capture the generally convex head component; a pole; an equator; a rim; and in which the spherical cap further comprises a plurality of resilient leaves defined by through-slits arranged circumferentially about the spherical cap, the through-slits extending longitudinally from the rim towards the pole. 2. The trial mobile bearing of claim 1, in which the through-slits are circumferentially spaced equally apart about the spherical cap. 3. The trial mobile bearing of claim 1 or 2, in which each through-slit extends longitudinally from the rim to at least the equator. 4. The trial mobile bearing of claim 1 or 2, in which each through-slit extends longitudinally from the rim to a position that is intermediate the equator and the pole. 5. The trial mobile bearing of any of claims 1 to 4, in which each through-slit has a first end and a second end, and in which there is a first distance (Di) between the pole and the second end of the through-slit when measured along the convex outer surface of the spherical cap, and a second distance (D 2 ) between the first end and the second end of the through-slit when measured along the convex outer surface, and in which Di is less than D 2 . 6. The trial mobile bearing of any of claims 1 to 4, in which each through-slit has a first end and a second end, and in which there is a first distance (Di) between the pole and the second end of the through-slit when measured along the convex outer surface of the spherical cap, and a second distance (D 2 ) between the first end and the second end of the through-slit when measured along the convex outer surface, and in which Di is equal to D 2 . 7. The trial mobile bearing of any of claims 1 to 4, in which each through-slit has a first end and a second end, and in which there is a first distance (Di) between the pole and the second end of the through-slit when measured along the convex outer surface of the spherical cap, and a second distance (D 2 ) between the first end and the second end of the through- slit when measured along the convex outer surface, and in which Di is greater than D 2 8. The trial mobile bearing of any of claims 5 to 7 in which D 2 of each through-slit is the same. 9. The trial mobile bearing of any preceding claim, in which each through-slit has a width when measured circumferentially about the spherical hollow cap, in which the width at the second end of the through-slit is greater than the width at the first end of the through-slit. 10. The trial mobile bearing of claim 9, in which the width of the through-slit at the second end is at least about 25% greater than the width of the through-slit at the first end. 11. The trial mobile bearing of claim 10, in which the width of the through-slit at the second end is between about 25% and about 75% greater than the width of the through-slit at the first end of the through-slit. 12. The trial mobile bearing of claim 11, in which the width of the through-slit at the second end is between about 25% and about 50% greater than the width of the through-slit at the first end of the through-slit. 13. The trial mobile bearing of any of claims 5 to 12, in which the second end of the through-slit is curved when viewed from the centre of the sphere. 14. The trial mobile bearing of any of claims 5 to 13, in which the second end of the through-slit is a generally bulbous shape. 15. The trial mobile bearing of any preceding claim, in which a hinge is provided on at least one resilient leaf of the plurality of resilient leaves. 16. The trial mobile bearing of claim 15, in which the hinge is provided by a locally thinned portion of the resilient leaf. 17. The trial mobile bearing of claim 16, in which the locally thinned portion defines a groove. 18. The trial mobile bearing of claim 17, in which the groove is provided on at least one of the inner concave surface and the outer convex surface. 19. The trial mobile bearing of any preceding claim, in which an aperture is provided at the pole. 20. A kit comprising a trial mobile bearing according to any of claims 1 to 19 and further comprising at least one of a trial femoral head component and a definitive femoral head component. 21. A kit comprising a plurality of trial mobile bearings of incrementally different sizes, each trial mobile bearing comprising: a spherical cap comprising a convex outer surface configured to engage with a generally concave inner bearing surface of a liner component; a concave inner surface configured to removably capture a generally convex head component; a pole, an equator; and a rim; 

 in which the spherical cap further comprises a plurality of resilient leaves defined by through-slits arranged circumferentially about the spherical cap, the through-slits extending longitudinally from the rim towards the pole. 22. The kit of claim 21, comprising a first trial mobile bearing and a second trial mobile bearing of a larger size, in which each trial bearing has a first distance (Di) between the pole and a second end of the through-slit when measured along the convex outer surface of the spherical cap, and a second distance (D 2 ) between a first end and the second end of the through-slit when measured along the convex outer surface, and a third distance (D 3 ) between the pole and the rim when measured along the convex outer surface of the spherical cap, in which D 3 is greater in the second trial mobile bearing than in the first trial mobile bearing, and in which the ratio of Di:D 2 is the same in the first trial mobile bearing as in the second trial mobile bearing. 23. A method of trialling comprising the steps of: (i) using a trial mobile bearing comprising: a spherical cap comprising: a convex outer surface configured to engage with a generally concave inner bearing surface of a liner component; a concave inner surface configured to removably capture a generally convex head component; a pole; an equator; and a rim; in which the spherical cap further comprises a plurality of resilient leaves defined by through-slits arranged circumferentially about the spherical cap, the through-slits extending longitudinally from the rim towards the pole, and (ii) inserting a generally convex head component into the trial mobile bearing. 24. A method of trialling according to claim 23, in which the cup component is an acetabular cup component and the generally convex head component is a femoral head component.
